topic: Someone discovers their partner has been writing a novel about them
raw_output:
[story 1]

The scent of aged paper and strong coffee hung heavy in the air of the study. I walked in, intending only to check on Mark, but the sight of the open laptop screen stopped me dead. The words swimming before my eyes were undeniably mine: my morning routine, the way I laughed when I was truly surprised, the specific shade of blue in my eyes when I’m concentrating. A cold knot tightened in my stomach, a sudden, sharp clenching beneath my ribs. I watched him, his brow furrowed in concentration, his hand poised over the keyboard, transforming my everyday existence into something grand, something fictional. When he finally looked up, his smile was warm, completely unaware of the seismic shift he’d just caused. “Hey, babe,” he said, his voice easy. I managed a tight nod, my throat suddenly dry. I needed to leave. Now.

[story 2]

I found the manuscript tucked beneath a stack of unrelated research notes. It was bound in worn leather, and the title, written in elegant script, was simply ‘The Architect of My Days.’ My hands trembled as I picked it up. Every sentence detailed moments I hadn't even realized I’d shared—the silent anxieties before a big meeting, the specific way I hum when I’m cooking. A heat began to rise in my cheeks, a flush that started at my neck and crept up to my hairline. I stood there for a long moment, the silence amplifying the frantic drumming in my ears. When he walked in, whistling tunelessly, I quickly slid the book under a heavy blanket. “Just some old drafts,” I mumbled, my voice sounding thin and strained. The air between us felt thick, charged with an unspoken tension that made my skin prickle.

[story 3]

It was an accident. I was looking for the remote and stumbled upon the folder labeled ‘Project Nightingale.’ Curiosity, or perhaps something sharper, pulled me closer. The first chapter was a perfect imitation of my own internal monologue, a breathtakingly accurate portrayal of my quiet moments. A wave of intense unease washed over me, making my vision swim slightly. I backed away slowly, my movements stiff, every muscle coiled tight. When he caught me staring, his expression shifted from relaxed to curious. “What’s got you?” he asked, his tone gentle, but I could feel the sudden, unwelcome need to retreat. I shook my head quickly, forcing a brittle smile. “Nothing. Just… a lot of reading.” The way he leaned in to kiss me felt suddenly intrusive, suffocating.
